name: From Santa Maria da Feira to Ovar
name_original: De Santa Maria da Feira a Ovar — percurso circular
description: This easy 15.5-mile (25.0 km) road cycling route from Santa Maria da Feira to Ovar offers coastal views, rural landscapes, and the Ria de Av
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Feira traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, lush forests, and agricultural land. The region offers a mix of quiet country roads and scenic paths, providing diverse terrain for cyclists. Elevations are generally moderate, with some routes featuring gentle climbs through wooded areas and open countryside. The area's varied topography supports a range of road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Feira The most popular road cycling route is From Santa Maria da Feira to Ovar, a 15.5 miles (25.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 11 minutes to complete. This easy route connects Santa Maria da Feira with the coastal town of Ovar. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ponte da Varela – Chapel Among Cornfields loop from Vila da Feira, a moderate 40.5 miles (65.2 km) path. This route features varied terrain, including open fields and small hamlets. Local road cyclists also love the Torreira Marina – Ponte da Varela loop from Vila da Feira, a 43.1 miles (69.3 km) trail leading through countryside and near riverine areas, often completed in about 3 hours 10 minutes. Road cycling around Feira is defined by rolling hills, lush forests, and agricultural land. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more moderate challenges. The routes in Feira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.4 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Feira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Feira?
A: The road cycling routes around Feira feature a diverse landscape of rolling hills, lush forests, and agricultural land. You'll find a mix of quiet country roads and scenic paths, with generally moderate elevations that include gentle climbs through wooded areas and open countryside.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Feira?
A: Yes, there are routes suitable for beginners. For an easier ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384223076/from-santa-maria-da-feira-to-ovar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Santa Maria da Feira to Ovar&lt;/a&gt; route, which is 15.5 miles (25.0 km) long and connects Santa Maria da Feira with the coastal town of Ovar.

Q: Can I find half-day road cycling routes in the Feira region?
A: Absolutely. Many routes are ideal for a half-day adventure. For example, the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384223076/from-santa-maria-da-feira-to-ovar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Santa Maria da Feira to Ovar&lt;/a&gt; route can be completed in just over an hour, making it perfect for a shorter ride. For a slightly longer, moderate option, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32206625" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Meeting Point – Monte da Abelheira loop from Cavaco&lt;/a&gt; is around 41.8 km and takes about 2 hours 10 minutes.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural features to look out for on road cycling routes?
A: The region offers picturesque scenery with rolling hills and lush forests. While cycling, you might encounter areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3130705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dune Forest of Ovar&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3130748" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pine sapping - Forest Trail&lt;/a&gt;. For more challenging ascents, the Serra de Montemuro (though further afield) offers rewarding panoramic views over the landscape.

Q: Are there any loop road cycling routes available around Feira?
A: Yes, there are several excellent loop routes. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19819158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte da Varela – Chapel Among Cornfields loop from Vila da Feira&lt;/a&gt;, covering 40.5 miles (65.2 km) with varied terrain. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32163127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreira Marina – Ponte da Varela loop from Vila da Feira&lt;/a&gt;, which is 43.1 miles (69.3 km) and leads through countryside and near riverine areas.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Feira?
A: Santa Maria da Feira benefits from a moderate climate, making it suitable for cycling throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer and the cooler, wetter days of winter.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Feira?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.4 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from quiet country roads to scenic paths, and the mix of rolling hills and lush forests that define the region's routes.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions near the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, you can find several points of interest. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/573237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrinha de Esmoriz Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt; offers natural beauty, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2924364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ovar Town Centre&lt;/a&gt; provides a cultural stop. You might also pass by the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6311238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Igreja Matriz de Oliveira de Azeméis&lt;/a&gt; on some routes.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Feira area?
A: Komoot currently lists over 15 road cycling routes in and around the Feira region, offering a range of options from easy to moderate difficulty levels.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: While most routes are moderate, some offer more significant elevation gains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32229351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel of Our Lord of the Rock – Aguda Dunes Park loop from Vila da Feira&lt;/a&gt;, a 66.4 km route, features over 550 meters of elevation gain, providing a good challenge for experienced cyclists.

Q: Where do road cycling routes typically start in the Feira region?
A: Many routes conveniently start from central locations like Santa Maria da Feira or Vila da Feira, making them easily accessible. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32206625" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Meeting Point – Monte da Abelheira loop&lt;/a&gt;, also start from smaller towns such as Cavaco.
tips: The Feira Castle is a landmark of medieval military architecture, standing out for its defensive diversity. Its history dates back to prehistory and pagan worship, with emphasis on the Reconquista and the Crisis of 1383-1385. The castle houses a chapel and a baroque chaplaincy house and was classified as a National Monument in 1910.

Ovar, blessed by nature, presents varied scenarios with the estuary, sea and forest. Highlights include Blue Flag beaches, the biodiversity of the Buçaquinho Environmental Park and outdoor activities such as surfing and stand-up paddleboarding. The cultural richness is evident in the decorative tiles and religious heritage, with emphasis on the Churches of Válega and Cortegaça. The municipality is also known for its vibrant cultural and gastronomic life, including the famous Pão-de-Ló de Ovar.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is classified as easy. The region around Ovar is generally known for its flat topography, making it suitable for a wide range of fitness levels. While moving inland towards Santa Maria da Feira, the terrain may become slightly more varied, it remains manageable for road cycling.

faqs.question: Is the 'From Santa Maria da Feira to Ovar' route a circular one?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route, meaning you will start and end in the same location, offering a convenient cycling experience without needing to arrange return transport.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route in Santa Maria da Feira?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the starting point are not provided, Santa Maria da Feira is a town with various parking options. It's advisable to look for public parking areas near the town center or close to the Castle of Santa Maria da Feira, which is a prominent landmark and potential starting point.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly flat or gently undulating, especially near Ovar and along the coast. Ovar boasts an extensive network of cycle paths, indicating good surfaces. Inland towards Santa Maria da Feira, you might encounter some rolling hills, but severe climbs are unlikely on this route. Road conditions are generally well-maintained, though cyclists should always be aware of traffic outside dedicated paths.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and natural features can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a blend of coastal and rural Portuguese landscapes. Near Ovar, expect scenic views of the Atlantic Ocean, dunes, and pine forests. The Ria de Aveiro, a shallow coastal lagoon, offers unique environmental beauty and diverse ecosystems. Further inland, towards Santa Maria da Feira, the scenery transitions to agricultural lands, small villages, and possibly some forested areas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region is generally pleasant for cycling for much of the year. Spring (April-June) and early autumn (September-October) typically offer mild temperatures and less crowded conditions, making them ideal. Summer can be warm, especially inland, but coastal breezes near Ovar can provide relief.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the 'From Santa Maria da Feira to Ovar' cycling route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the historic Castle of Santa Maria da Feira at the start or end of your journey. As you approach Ovar, you'll find the Ovar Town Centre, known for its extensive cycle paths. The route also offers opportunities to enjoy the natural beauty of the Ria de Aveiro and coastal areas with beaches like Furadouro.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local cycling paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Percurso Urbano do Cáster, Ciclovias de Ovar, and Ciclovia da Avenida Doutor Francisco Sá Carneiro. It also utilizes smaller local roads like Rua Porto Junco and Caminho da Ronda, integrating into the broader cycling network of the area.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact route are not detailed, many public paths in Portugal allow dogs on a leash. Given that parts of the route are on dedicated cycle paths and rural roads, it's generally possible to bring a well-behaved dog on a leash. However, always be mindful of local signage and other cyclists.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and established cycle paths, which are freely accessible to cyclists.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Santa Maria da Feira and Ovar are towns with various cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. Along the route, especially as you pass through smaller villages or approach the town centers, you'll find opportunities to stop for refreshments. Ovar, in particular, is well-equipped for cyclists.

faqs.question: What should I consider packing for this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: For this racebike tour, pack essentials like a helmet, water bottles, basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and appropriate cycling attire. Sunscreen and sunglasses are recommended, especially during warmer months. Given the potential for stops in towns, a small lock for your bike could be useful.

waypoints.name: Castle of Santa Maria da Feira
